text: The surgeon performs a limited dissection of the upper pole to remove attachments, followed by further dissection of the colon, which is mobilized medially. A window is created at the lateral fold of the peritoneum (L-FOR), and the ureter and gonadal vein are elevated. The dissection is carried upward towards the renal hilum.

The surgeon performs a dissection of the lower pole of the kidney, freeing it from surrounding tissues. The adrenal hilum is carefully dissected, and the adrenal gland is mobilized superiorly. The adrenal vein is identified, clipped, and divided.
The surgeon meticulously dissects the mass, separating it from the undersurface of the liver. The renal artery is clipped using a Hem-o-lok clip and then divided.

The surgeon removes dense adhesions surrounding the renal vein and divides the vein between clips. Urated clips are applied and divided. The specimen is placed into a retrieval bag and extracted through a financial incision.
